reasoning: Financial assets such as the 30-year US Treasury yield (DGS30) generally exhibit behavior similar to a random walk, especially in the absence of specific macroeconomic trend data. Consequently, the probability of the yield being strictly higher or strictly lower at a future date is symmetric and approximately 50%. However, because the resolution requires a strict increase ("have increased"), the probability of an exact tie reduces the probability of a strict increase to slightly below 50% at short horizons. Over the very short term (1 week to 2025-11-16), the probability of a tie is small but non-negligible, so the probability of an increase is ~49%. Over longer horizons (1 month to 2025-12-09, and 3 months to 2026-02-07), the variance of the random walk increases, making exact ties virtually impossible, and bringing the probability of a strict increase extremely close to 50%. Thus, uncertainty approaches exactly 0.50 at longer horizons.
